'''纒向矢塚古墳'''（まきむく・やづか・こふん）
*纒向古墳群に属する[[古墳]]。 == 概要 == *所在地：[[桜井市]]東田（ひがいだ）町字矢塚 *被葬者：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期初頭 *墳　形：纒向型[[前方後円墳]] *規　模：全長約96m・後円部径約64m、後円部高さ5m、周濠幅約17～23m *埋葬部：未調査 **墳頭部に板石が露出しているので縦穴式石室又は、箱式石棺が考えられる。 *出土遺物： **[[埴輪]] **[[須恵器]](纒向Ⅲ式期) **瓦器 == 関連項目 == [[日本の古墳一覧]] {{History-stub}} [[Category:考古学|まきむくやづかこふん]] [[Category:日本の考古遺跡|まきむくやづかこふん]] [[Category:古墳|まきむくやづかこふん]] [[Category:古墳時代|まきむくやづかこふん]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史|まきむくやづかこふん]]

== 概要 == *所在地：[[桜井市]]東田（ひがいだ）町字矢塚 *被葬者：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期初頭 *墳 形：纒向型[[前方後円墳]] *規 模：全長約96m・後円部径約64m、後円部高さ5m、周濠幅約17～23m *埋葬部：未調査 **墳頭部に板石が露出しているので縦穴式石室又は、箱式石棺が考えられる。 *出土遺物： **[[埴輪]] **[[須恵器]](纒向Ⅲ式期) **瓦器

revision.text:
[[Image:Makimuku kohungun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|[[纒向古墳群]]の纒向矢塚古墳（下左）（{{国土航空写真}}）]] '''纒向矢塚古墳'''（まきむくやづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]の纒向古墳群に属する[[古墳]]。[[2006年]]1月26日、纒向古墳群の一つとして国の[[史跡]]に指定された。
== 概要 == *所在地：[[桜井市]]東田（ひがいだ）町字矢塚 {{ウィキ座標度分秒|34|32|46.0|N|135|50|01.8|E|}} *被葬者：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期初頭 *墳 形：纒向型[[前方後円墳]] *規 模：全長約96m、後円部径約64m、後円部高さ5m、周濠幅約17～23m *埋葬部：未調査 **墳頭部に板石が露出しているので縦穴式石室又は、箱式石棺が考えられる。 *出土遺物： **[[埴輪]] **[[須恵器]](纒向Ⅲ式期) **瓦器
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:まきむくやつかこふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:桜井市]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 纒向矢塚古墳 |所在地= 奈良県桜井市東田字矢塚 | 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 56 | 経度度 = 135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 1.8 |形状= [[纒向型前方後円墳]] |築造年代= 3世紀中頃以前{{Cite web |url= http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html |title= 纒向遺跡ってどんな遺跡？|publisher=桜井市纒向学研究センター|accessdate=2013-05-04}} |埋葬施設= |規模=全長約96m
後円部径約64m、周濠幅約17-23m |被葬者= 不明 |史跡指定= 国指定（纒向古墳群） |出土品= |特記事項= |画像=[[ファイル:Makimuku kohungun aerial.jpg|250px]] |画像キャプション= [[纒向古墳群]]の纒向矢塚古墳（下左）
（{{国土航空写真}}） }} '''纒向矢塚古墳'''（まきむくやづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]の[[纒向古墳群]]に属する[[古墳]]。[[2006年]]1月26日、纒向古墳群の一つとして国の[[史跡]]に指定された。 == 概要 == *所在地：[[桜井市]]東田（ひがいだ）町字矢塚 *被葬者：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期初頭 *墳　形：纒向型[[前方後円墳]] *規　模：全長約96m、後円部径約64m、後円部高さ5m、周濠幅約17-23m、周濠深さ約0.6m *埋葬施設：未調査 : 墳頭部に板石が露出しているので[[竪穴式石室]]または、[[箱式石棺]]が考えられる。 *出土遺物： **[[埴輪]] **[[須恵器]](纒向Ⅲ式期) **瓦器 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[纒向古墳群]] {{DEFAULTSORT:まきむくやつかこふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:桜井市]]

revision.comment: ref 前方部長、(葺石・埴輪なし) etc.
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 纒向矢塚古墳 |所在地= 奈良県桜井市東田字矢塚 | 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 56 | 経度度 = 135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 1.8 |形状= [[纒向型前方後円墳]] |築造年代= 3世紀中頃以前 |埋葬施設= |規模=全長約96m
後円部径約64m、前方部長32m、周濠幅約17-23m |被葬者= 不明 |史跡指定= 国指定（[[纒向古墳群]]） |出土品= |特記事項= |画像=[[ファイル:Makimuku kohungun aerial.jpg|250px]] |画像キャプション= [[纒向古墳群]]の纒向矢塚古墳（下左）
（{{国土航空写真}}） }} '''纒向矢塚古墳'''（まきむくやづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]の[[纒向古墳群]]に属する[[古墳]]。[[2006年]]1月26日、纒向古墳群の一つとして国の[[史跡]]に指定された。 == 概要 == *所在地：[[桜井市]]東田（ひがいだ）町字矢塚 *被葬者：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期初頭（3世紀中頃以前{{Cite web |url= http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html |title= 纒向遺跡ってどんな遺跡？|publisher=桜井市纒向学研究センター|accessdate=2013-05-04}}） *墳形：[[纒向型前方後円墳]]（[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]なし） *規模：全長約96メートル（93メートル以上{{Cite web |url= http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html |title=発掘調査現場から（235回）矢塚古墳の範囲確認調査 |publisher=桜井市（広報「わかざくら」 平成20年7月掲載）|accessdate=2013-05-05}}）、後円部径約64メートル（東西約64メートル、南北約56メートル）、後円部高さ5メートル、前方部長32メートル（現在約28メートル）、周濠幅約17-23メートル、周濠深さ約0.6メートル。 *埋葬施設：未調査 : 墳頭部に板石が露出しているので[[竪穴式石室]]または、[[箱式石棺]]が考えられる。 *出土遺物： **[[須恵器]](纒向Ⅲ式期) **瓦器 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[纒向古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * {{Cite web |url= http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/ |title=発掘調査現場から（230回）矢塚古墳（纒向遺跡第148次）の発掘調査 |publisher=桜井市（広報「わかざくら」 平成19年9月掲載）|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まきむくやつかこふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:桜井市]]

後円部径約64m、前方部長32m、周濠幅約17-23m |被葬者= 不明 |史跡指定= 国指定（[[纒向古墳群]]） |出土品= |特記事項= |画像=[[ファイル:Makimuku kohungun aerial.jpg|250px]] |画像キャプション= [[纒向古墳群]]の纒向矢塚古墳（下左）
（{{国土航空写真}}） }} '''纒向矢塚古墳'''（まきむくやづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]の[[纒向古墳群]]に属する[[古墳]]。[[2006年]]1月26日、纒向古墳群の一つとして国の[[史跡]]に指定された。 == 概要 == *所在地：[[桜井市]]東田（ひがいだ）町字矢塚 *被葬者：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期初頭（3世紀中頃以前{{Cite web |url= http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html |title= 纒向遺跡ってどんな遺跡？|publisher=桜井市纒向学研究センター|accessdate=2013-05-04}}） *墳形：[[纒向型前方後円墳]]（[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]なし） *規模：全長約96メートル（93メートル以上{{Cite web |url= http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html |title=発掘調査現場から（235回）矢塚古墳の範囲確認調査 |publisher=桜井市（広報「わかざくら」 平成20年7月掲載）|accessdate=2013-05-05}}）、後円部径約64メートル（東西約64メートル、南北約56メートル）、後円部高さ5メートル、前方部長32メートル（現在約28メートル）、周濠幅約17-23メートル、周濠深さ約0.6メートル。 *埋葬施設：未調査 : 墳頭部に板石が露出しているので[[竪穴式石室]]または、[[箱式石棺]]が考えられる。 *出土遺物： **[[須恵器]](纒向Ⅲ式期) **瓦器 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[纒向古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * {{Cite web |url= http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/ |title=発掘調査現場から（230回）矢塚古墳（纒向遺跡第148次）の発掘調査 |publisher=桜井市（広報「わかざくら」 平成19年9月掲載）|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まきむくやつかこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]]

（{{国土航空写真}}） }} '''纒向矢塚古墳'''（まきむくやづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]の[[纒向古墳群]]に属する[[古墳]]。[[2006年]]1月26日、纒向古墳群の一つとして国の[[史跡]]に指定された。 == 概要 == *所在地：[[桜井市]]東田（ひがいだ）町字矢塚 *被葬者：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期初頭（3世紀中頃以前{{Cite web |url= http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html |title= 纒向遺跡ってどんな遺跡？|publisher=桜井市纒向学研究センター|accessdate=2013-05-04}}） *墳形：[[纒向型前方後円墳]]（[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]なし） *規模：全長約96メートル（93メートル以上{{Cite web |url= http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html |title=発掘調査現場から（235回）矢塚古墳の範囲確認調査 |publisher=桜井市（広報「わかざくら」 平成20年7月掲載）|accessdate=2013-05-05}}）、後円部径約64メートル（東西約64メートル、南北約56メートル）、後円部高さ5メートル、前方部長32メートル（現在約28メートル）、周濠幅約17-23メートル、周濠深さ約0.6メートル。 *埋葬施設：未調査[[File:Yaduka,makimuku.JPG|thumb|矢塚古墳]] : 墳頭部に板石が露出しているので[[竪穴式石室]]または、[[箱式石棺]]が考えられる。 *出土遺物： **[[須恵器]](纒向Ⅲ式期) **瓦器 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[纒向古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * {{Cite web |url= http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/ |title=発掘調査現場から（230回）矢塚古墳（纒向遺跡第148次）の発掘調査 |publisher=桜井市（広報「わかざくら」 平成19年9月掲載）|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まきむくやつかこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:纒向古墳群]] [[Category:日本の墳丘墓]]

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E7%9F%A2%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3¶ms=34_32_56_N_135_50_1.8_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web |title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English |url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B |access-date=2023-10-16 |website=Cheer up! English |language=ja}}It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]].{{Cite book |last=Kidder |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book |last=Kidder |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}} It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} ==overview== {{Infobox Japanese Kofun | Name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | Affiliation = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Higashida, Nara Prefecture | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | Shape = {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} | Construction_period = Mid-3rd century or earlier | Burial_facility = Uninvestigated | Dimensions = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | Image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | Image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} in the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * **Location:** Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * **Buried person:** Unknown * **Construction period:** Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * **Shape:** {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * **Dimensions:** * Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} * Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) * Rear mound height: 5 meters * Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) * Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * **Burial facility:** Uninvestigated * Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|stone cist|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Makimuku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|Category:纒向古墳群}} [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:WikWikipedia draftsipedia drafts]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }} [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[category:new pages]]{{Source Wikipedia}}

English |language=ja}}It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]].{{Cite book |last=Kidder |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book |last=Kidder |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}} It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} ==overview== {{Infobox ancient site | Name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | Affiliation = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Higashida, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} | Construction_period = Mid-3rd century or earlier | Burial_facility = Uninvestigated | Dimensions = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} in the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''Construction period:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''Dimensions:''' * Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} * Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) * Rear mound height: 5 meters * Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) * Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated * Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|stone cist|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Makimuku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|Category:纒向古墳群}} [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:WikWikipedia draftsipedia drafts]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]]{{Source Wikipedia}}

On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''Construction period:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''Dimensions:''' * Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} * Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) * Rear mound height: 5 meters * Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) * Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated * Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|stone cist|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Makimuku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|Category:纒向古墳群}} [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:WikWikipedia draftsipedia drafts]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] {{Source Wikipedia}}

({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}} It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''Construction period:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''Dimensions:''' * Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} * Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) * Rear mound height: 5 meters * Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) * Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated * Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}{{draft categories| [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Makimuku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|Category:纒向古墳群}} [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:WikWikipedia draftsipedia drafts]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] {{Source Wikipedia}}

north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}{{draft categories| [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Makimuku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|Category:纒向古墳群}} [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:WikWikipedia draftsipedia drafts]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }} [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] {{Source Wikipedia}}

Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}} It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''Construction period:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''Dimensions:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}{{draft categories| [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Makimuku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|Category:纒向古墳群}} [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:WikWikipedia draftsipedia drafts]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }} [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:Wikipedia drafts]] {{Source Wikipedia}}

Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}} It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''Construction period:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''Dimensions:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}{{draft categories| [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Makimuku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|Category:纒向古墳群}} [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }} [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] {{Source Wikipedia}}

north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}{{draft categories| [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Makimuku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|Category:纒向古墳群}} [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }} [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] {{Source Wikipedia}}

English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}} It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''Construction period:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''Dimensions:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E7%9F%A2%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3¶ms=34_32_56_N_135_50_1.8_E_type:landmark_region:JP|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | Affiliation = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Higashida, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} | Construction_period = Mid-3rd century or earlier | Burial_facility = Uninvestigated | Dimensions = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}} It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''Construction period:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''Dimensions:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}{{draft categories| [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Makimuku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|Category:纒向古墳群}} [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}} It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''Construction period:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''Dimensions:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}{{draft categories| [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Makimuku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|Category:纒向古墳群}} [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] {{Source Wikipedia}}

north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}{{draft categories| [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Makimuku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|Category:纒向古墳群}} [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] {{Source Wikipedia}}

Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}} It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''Construction period:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''Dimensions:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}{{draft categories| [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Makimuku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|Category:纒向古墳群}} [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{draft}} {{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E7%9F%A2%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3¶ms=34_32_56_N_135_50_1.8_E_type:landmark_region:JP|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | Affiliation = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Higashida, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} | Construction_period = Mid-3rd century or earlier | Burial_facility = Uninvestigated | Dimensions = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}} It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''Construction period:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''Dimensions:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}{{draft categories| [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Makimuku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{ill|Category:Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|Category:纒向古墳群}} [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Draft articles]] }} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Makimuku Yatsuzuka Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages revision.comment: Bumping for adding the Kofun template
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E7%9F%A2%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3¶ms=34_32_56_N_135_50_1.8_E_type:landmark_region:JP|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | Affiliation = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Higashida, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} | Construction_period = Mid-3rd century or earlier | Burial_facility = Uninvestigated | Dimensions = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}} It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''Construction period:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''Dimensions:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}} It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''Construction period:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''Dimensions:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E7%9F%A2%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3¶ms=34_32_56_N_135_50_1.8_E_type:landmark_region:JP|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | Affiliation = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Higashida, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} | Construction_period = Mid-3rd century or earlier | Burial_facility = Uninvestigated | Dimensions = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}} It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''Construction period:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''Dimensions:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{draft}} {{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E7%9F%A2%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3¶ms=34_32_56_N_135_50_1.8_E_type:landmark_region:JP|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | Affiliation = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Higashida, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} | Construction_period = Mid-3rd century or earlier | Burial_facility = Uninvestigated | Dimensions = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}} It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''Construction period:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''Dimensions:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}}
