revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, dd February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E7%9F%A2%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3¶ms=34_32_56_N_135_50_1.8_E_type:landmark_region:JP|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | Affiliation = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Higashida, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} | Construction_period = Mid-3rd century or earlier | Burial_facility = Uninvestigated | Dimensions = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''Construction period:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''Dimensions:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Sakurai]] revision.comment: Text replacement - "|須恵器}}" to "|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, dd February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E7%9F%A2%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3¶ms=34_32_56_N_135_50_1.8_E_type:landmark_region:JP|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | Affiliation = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Higashida, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} | Construction_period = Mid-3rd century or earlier | Burial_facility = Uninvestigated | Dimensions = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''Construction period:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''Dimensions:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] revision.comment: doing more mass replacements to fix the issues
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, dd February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E7%9F%A2%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3¶ms=34_32_56_N_135_50_1.8_E_type:landmark_region:JP|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | Affiliation = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Higashida, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} | Construction_period = Mid-3rd century or earlier | Burial_facility = Uninvestigated | Dimensions = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''Dimensions:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial Facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of ({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, ({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial Facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == burial goods == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Sakurai]] ({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial Facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == burial goods == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Sakurai]] Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''Burial Facility:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == burial goods == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Sakurai]] Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == burial goods == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Sakurai]] web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == burial goods == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Sakurai]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, dd February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E7%9F%A2%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3¶ms=34_32_56_N_135_50_1.8_E_type:landmark_region:JP|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | Affiliation = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Higashida, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} | Construction_period = Mid-3rd century or earlier | Burial_facility = Uninvestigated | area = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == burial goods == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Sakurai]] ({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == burial goods == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Sakurai]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]]. Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National other_designation ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National other_designation as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == burial goods == Artifacts from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Sakurai]] revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, dd February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E7%9F%A2%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3¶ms=34_32_56_N_135_50_1.8_E_type:landmark_region:JP|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Higashida, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} | Construction_period = Mid-3rd century or earlier | burial = Uninvestigated | area = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National other_designation ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designated as a National other_designation as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Sakurai]] Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Buried_person = Unknown | Designated_site = National other_designation ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designation1 as a National other_designation as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Sakurai]] Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designation1 as a National other_designation as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Sakurai]] revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, dd February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E7%9F%A2%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3¶ms=34_32_56_N_135_50_1.8_E_type:landmark_region:JP|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Higashida, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} | built = Mid-3rd century or earlier | burial = Uninvestigated | area = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Interred_individual = Unknown | Designated_site = National other_designation ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was designation1 as a National other_designation as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the ({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was other_designation1 as a National other_other_designation as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Buried person:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} *
