{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, dd February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E7%9F%A2%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3¶ms=34_32_56_N_135_50_1.8_E_type:landmark_region:JP|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市|12=draft}}, Higashida, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=draft}} | built = Mid-3rd century or earlier | burial = Uninvestigated | area = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Interred_individual = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|12=draft}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=draft}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|12=draft}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[ceb:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd to dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Makimuku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages with interlanguage linked categories]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Group]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Makimuku Yatsuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] [[sn:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}}

({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|12=draft}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=draft}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|12=draft}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[ceb:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Makimuku Yatsuka revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Unsorted]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, dd February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E7%9F%A2%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3¶ms=34_32_56_N_135_50_1.8_E_type:landmark_region:JP|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市|12=draft}}, Higashida, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=draft}} | built = Mid-3rd century or earlier | burial = Uninvestigated | area = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Interred_individual = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|12=draft}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=draft}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|12=draft}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[ceb:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd to dd]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Makimuku Yatsuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Interred_individual = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft|12=draft}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|12=draft|12=draft}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[ceb:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd to dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Makimuku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages with interlanguage linked categories]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Group]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|12=draft|12=draft}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[ceb:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, dd February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E7%9F%A2%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3¶ms=34_32_56_N_135_50_1.8_E_type:landmark_region:JP|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Higashida, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=draft|12=draft}} | built = Mid-3rd century or earlier | burial = Uninvestigated | area = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Interred_individual = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft|12=draft}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|12=draft|12=draft}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[ceb:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever-->"

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, dd February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E7%9F%A2%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3¶ms=34_32_56_N_135_50_1.8_E_type:landmark_region:JP|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Higashida, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} | built = Mid-3rd century or earlier | burial = Uninvestigated | area = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Interred_individual = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|纒向古墳群}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[ceb:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Makimuku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages with interlanguage linked categories]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Group]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Makimuku Yatsuzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]]

({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashidaotsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida-ōtsuka Kofun|ja|東田大塚古墳|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳}} (no stone facing or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Group|ja|纒向古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[ceb:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| revision.comment: 23 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:纒向矢塚古墳 up to rev 99047690
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 纒向矢塚古墳 |所属=[[纒向古墳群]] |所在地= [[奈良県]][[桜井市]]東田字矢塚 | 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 56 | 経度度 = 135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 1.8 |形状= [[纒向型前方後円墳]] |築造年代= 3世紀中頃以前 |埋葬施設= |規模=全長約96[[メートル|m]]
後円部径約64m、前方部長32m、周濠幅約17-23m |被葬者= 不明 |史跡指定= 国指定（[[纒向古墳群]]） |出土品= |特記事項= |画像=[[ファイル:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] |画像キャプション= [[纒向古墳群]]の纒向矢塚古墳（下左）
（{{国土航空写真}}） }} '''纒向矢塚古墳'''（まきむくやづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]の[[纒向古墳群]]に属する[[古墳]]。[[2006年]]1月26日、纒向古墳群の一つとして国の[[史跡]]に指定された。 == 概要 == *所在地：[[桜井市]]東田（ひがいだ）町字矢塚 *被葬者：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期初頭（3世紀中頃以前{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html |title= 纒向遺跡ってどんな遺跡？|publisher=桜井市纒向学研究センター|accessdate=2013-05-04}}） *墳形：[[纒向型前方後円墳]]（[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]なし） *規模：全長約96[[メートル]]（93メートル以上{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html |title=発掘調査現場から（235回）矢塚古墳の範囲確認調査 |publisher=桜井市（広報「わかざくら」 平成20年7月掲載）|accessdate=2013-05-05}}）、後円部径約64メートル（東西約64メートル、南北約56メートル）、後円部高さ5メートル、前方部長32メートル（現在約28メートル）、周濠幅約17-23メートル、周濠深さ約0.6メートル。 *埋葬施設：未調査[[File:Yaduka,makimuku.JPG|thumb|矢塚古墳]] : 墳頭部に板石が露出しているので[[竪穴式石室]]または、[[箱式石棺]]が考えられる。 *出土遺物： **[[須恵器]](纒向Ⅲ式期) **瓦器 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[纒向古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * {{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/ |title=発掘調査現場から（230回）矢塚古墳（纒向遺跡第148次）の発掘調査 |publisher=桜井市（広報「わかざくら」 平成19年9月掲載）|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まきむくやつかこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:纒向古墳群]] [[Category:日本の墳丘墓]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, dd February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E7%9F%A2%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3¶ms=34_32_56_N_135_50_1.8_E_type:landmark_region:JP|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | part_of = [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], Higashida, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped Kofuns|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped Kofuns|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} | built = Mid-3rd century or earlier | burial = Uninvestigated | area = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Interred_individual = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ([[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Cluster]]) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | caption = The [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|qq=}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Group]], located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} [[:en:Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun]] is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped Kofuns|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped Kofuns|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} (no stone facing or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Group]] == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|纒向矢塚古墳|version=99047690|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai]] [[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:Makimuku]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Group]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] [[ceb:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] [[sn:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] [[en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]]

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, dd February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E7%9F%A2%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3¶ms=34_32_56_N_135_50_1.8_E_type:landmark_region:JP|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | part_of = [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], Higashida, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{coordinates|34|32|56|N|135|50|1.8|E}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped Kofuns|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped Kofuns|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} | built = Mid-3rd century or earlier | burial = Uninvestigated | area = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Interred_individual = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ([[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Cluster]]) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | image = [[File:Three Makimuku kofun aerial view.jpg|250px]] | caption = The [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|qq=}}) }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Group]], located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} [[:en:Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun]] is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped Kofuns|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped Kofuns|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} (no stone facing or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Group]] == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|纒向矢塚古墳|version=99047690|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] [[ceb:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] [[sn:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Group]], located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} [[:en:Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun]] is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped Kofuns|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped Kofuns|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} (no stone facing or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Group]] == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|纒向矢塚古墳|version=99047690|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with successful]][[sn:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] [[ceb:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] [[sn:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped Kofuns|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped Kofuns|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} (no stone facing or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakogata sekkan (box-shaped stone coffin)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki.
