Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, dd February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Yaduka,makimuku.JPG|coordinates={{Coord|34.548889|N|135.833833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | part_of = [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] | location = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} | built = Mid-3rd century or earlier | burial = Uninvestigated | area = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Interred_individual = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ([[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Cluster]]) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | caption = The [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}) | architectural_styles = {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11608504}} }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Group]], located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} [[:en:Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun]] is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} (no stone facing or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Group]] == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|纒向矢塚古墳|version=99047690|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun}} [[ceb:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Yayoi burial mounds]] ({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}) | architectural_styles = {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11608504}} }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Group]], located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} [[:en:Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun]] is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} (no stone facing or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Group]] == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|纒向矢塚古墳|version=99047690|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Tumuli in Japan]] [[Category:Yamatai]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun}} [[ceb:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Yayoi burial mounds]] 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
[[en:Makimuku Yazuka Kofun]][[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] [[en:Makimuku Yazuka Kofun]][[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] [[en:Makimuku Yazuka Kofun]][[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11608503]] Wikidata name: Makimuku Yazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, dd February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Yaduka,makimuku.JPG|coordinates={{Coord|34.548889|N|135.833833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | part_of = [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] | location = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} | built = Mid-3rd century or earlier | burial = Uninvestigated | area = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Interred_individual = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ([[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Cluster]]) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | caption = The [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}) | architectural_styles = {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11608504}} }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Group]], located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} [[:en:Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun]] is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} (no stone facing or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Group]] == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|纒向矢塚古墳|version=99047690|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{everybodywiki link|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun}} [[ceb:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] [[en:Makimuku Yazuka Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11608503}} [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, dd February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Yaduka,makimuku.JPG|coordinates={{Coord|34.548889|N|135.833833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | part_of = [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] | location = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} | built = Mid-3rd century or earlier | burial = Uninvestigated | area = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Interred_individual = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ([[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Cluster]]) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | caption = The [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}) | architectural_styles = {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11608504}} }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Group]], located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} [[:en:Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun]] is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} (no stone facing or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Makimuku Kofun Cluster|Makimuku Kofun Group]] == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|纒向矢塚古墳|version=99047690|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun}} [[ceb:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] [[en:Makimuku Yazuka Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11608503}} [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mount Miwa Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, dd February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Yaduka,makimuku.JPG|coordinates={{Coord|34.548889|N|135.833833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} | built = Mid-3rd century or earlier | burial = Uninvestigated | area = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Interred_individual = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}) | architectural_styles = {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11608504}} }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|WD=Q11528075|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} (no stone facing or {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated revision.text:
[[en:Makimuku Yazuka Kofun]][[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] [[en:Makimuku Yazuka Kofun]][[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] [[en:Makimuku Yazuka Kofun]][[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{afc comment|1=to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun|Makimuku Yatsuka Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Yaduka,makimuku.JPG|coordinates={{Coord|34.548889|N|135.833833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} | built = Mid-3rd century or earlier | burial = Uninvestigated | area = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Interred_individual = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}) | architectural_styles = {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11608504}} }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|WD=Q11528075|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} (no stone facing or {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated revision.text:
[[en:Makimuku Yazuka Kofun]][[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] [[en:Makimuku Yazuka Kofun]][[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] [[en:Makimuku Yazuka Kofun]][[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Yaduka,makimuku.JPG|coordinates={{Coord|34.548889|N|135.833833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} | built = Mid-3rd century or earlier | burial = Uninvestigated | area = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Interred_individual = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}) | architectural_styles = {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11608504}} }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|WD=Q11528075|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} (no stone facing or {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a [[en:Makimuku Yazuka Kofun]][[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] [[en:Makimuku Yazuka Kofun]][[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] [[en:Makimuku Yazuka Kofun]][[ja:纒向矢塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Yaduka,makimuku.JPG|coordinates={{Coord|34.548889|N|135.833833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|Infobox ancient site | name = Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (纒向矢塚古墳) | part_of = {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|1=Makimuku Kofun Cluster}} | location = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} | type = {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} | built = Mid-3rd century or earlier | burial = Uninvestigated | area = Approx. 96 meters in length
Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters
Front mound length: 32 meters
Moat width: 17–23 meters | Interred_individual = Unknown | Designated_site = National Historic Site ({{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|1=Makimuku Kofun Cluster}}) | Excavated_items = | Additional_notes = | caption = The {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Cluster|1=Makimuku Kofun Cluster}} with Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun (bottom left)
({{ill|GSI Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/GSI Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397|1=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan}}) | architectural_styles = {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11608504|1=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun}} }} '''Makimuku Yadzuka Kofun''' (纒向矢塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]https://www.rekihaku.ac.jp/english/outline/publication/ronbun/ronbun7/pdf/163004.pdf.{{Cite web|title=「纒,向,矢,塚,古墳,まき,むく,づか,奈良,県,桜井,市,群,属する」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|1=Makimuku Kofun Cluster}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|WD=Q11528075|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} (no stone facing or {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * {{ill|Sue the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} (no stone facing or {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|1=Makimuku Kofun Cluster}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} English|url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=%E7%BA%92+%E5%90%91+%E7%9F%A2+%E5%A1%9A+%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3+%E3%81%BE%E3%81%8D+%E3%82%80%E3%81%8F+%E3%81%A5%E3%81%8B+%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF+%E7%9C%8C+%E6%A1%9C%E4%BA%95+%E5%B8%82+%E7%BE%A4+%E5%B1%9E%E3%81%99%E3%82%8B|access-date=2023-10-16|website=Cheer up! English|language=ja}} It is a member of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|1=Makimuku Kofun Cluster}}, located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|qid=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan.{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|249}} {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|qid=Q11528075|lt=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|1=Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun}} is located to the north of it..{{Cite book|last=Kidder|first=J. Edward|url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848|title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai|date=2017-12-18|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}{{rp|251}}It's on the northwestern part of the group..{{rp|251}} On January 26, 2006, it was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site as part of the Makimuku Kofun Cluster. == Overview == * '''location:''' Higashida, Sakurai City, Nara Prefecture * '''Interred_individual:''' Unknown * '''built:''' Early Kofun period (mid-3rd century or earlier) {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html|title=What is the Makimuku Site?|publisher=Sakurai City Makimuku Research Center|accessdate=2013-05-04}} * '''type:''' {{ill|Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|lt=Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku-style keyhole-shaped tumulus|qq=}} (no stone facing or {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}) * '''area:''' ** Overall length: approx. 96 meters (at least 93 meters){{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai12.html|title=Fieldwork Reports (235th): Confirmation Survey of Yadzuka Kofun|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, July 2008)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} ** Rear mound diameter: approx. 64 meters (east-west: approx. 64 meters; north-south: approx. 56 meters) ** Rear mound height: 5 meters ** Front mound length: 32 meters (current length: approx. 28 meters) ** Moat width: 17–23 meters; depth: approx. 0.6 meters * '''burial:''' Uninvestigated ** Stone slabs visible at the top suggest the presence of either a {{ill|vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} or a {{ill|Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|lt=Hakogata sekkan|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakogata sekkan (box-type Sarcophagus)|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}. == burial goods == burial goods from the site include: * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (Makimuku III period) * Pottery fragments == notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11608501|lt=Makimuku Kofun Group|1=Makimuku Kofun Cluster}} == External Links == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/bunkazai/|title=Fieldwork Reports (230th): Excavation of Yadzuka Kofun (Makimuku Site 148th Survey)|publisher=Sakurai City ("Wakazakura" Newsletter, September 2007)|accessdate=2013-05-05}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}}
